name: Atayal
notes: Atayal (Tayal) — Formosan (Austronesian) language of northern Taiwan. The map commits to the OFFICIAL ROMANISATION of the Council of Indigenous Peoples / Ministry of Education, 原住民族語言書寫系統 (15 December 2005), Atayal writing system (covering the Squliq, Skikun, Ciʼuli, Matuʼuwal/Mayrinax and Plngawan dialects). Letters whose value departs from the IPA: ⟨b⟩ = /β/ (~[v]), ⟨c⟩ = /ts/, ⟨g⟩ = /ɣ/, ⟨h⟩ = the voiceless PHARYNGEAL fricative /ħ/ (Li 1980; realised [h] by some speakers), ⟨x⟩ = /x/, ⟨y⟩ = /j/, ⟨z⟩ = /z/, ⟨ng⟩ = /ŋ/, and the apostrophe ⟨ʼ⟩ = /ʔ/. The official standard writes ⟨n_g⟩ for a /n/+/ɣ/ sequence, to disambiguate it from ⟨ng⟩ = /ŋ/. ⟨r⟩ is the alveolar trill /r/ in most dialects but the flap /ɾ/ in Plngawan (Wanda) Atayal, so both are listed. Vowels ⟨i e a u o⟩ = /i e a u o/; the letter ⟨e⟩ also covers the schwa /ə/ found in most dialects (Plngawan lacks it), and in several dialects the schwa is simply not written, producing long surface consonant clusters (e.g. pspngun /pəsəpəŋun/) — such omitted vowels are NOT recoverable from the spelling. /s/ and /ts/ are palatalised to [ɕ tɕ] before /i/ and /j/ (allophones). DIALECT SCOPE: a single letter→IPA table across dialects; where the dialects differ (⟨q⟩ is absent from Skikun, ⟨z⟩ from Yilan Ciʼuli) the letter simply does not occur in that dialect's text. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Atayal in the 2005 official orthography; older missionary or ⟨ḳ č š ž⟩ conventions are out of scope.

sources.author: Council of Indigenous Peoples; Ministry of Education (Republic of China)
sources.title: 原住民族語言書寫系統 (Writing Systems for the Indigenous Languages)
sources.notes: Official orthography promulgated 2005-12-15; section (二) 泰雅語書寫系統 gives the 20 consonant and 5 vowel letters with their IPA values.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Writing systems of Formosan languages
sources.notes: Consolidated letter→IPA table for the 16 officially recognised Formosan languages, plus the 2016-2018 revision decisions.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
